heading: Pyth-2 page-75 Quantum Prime notation promoted into the validated lane
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, page75, prime-numbering, validated-workset]
body: Continued the page-75 notation recovery until the evidence threshold was strong enough to promote `pyth2_p075_l5663` into the corrected-source validated lane. The key upgrade was a clean local notation witness from `qa-2__001_qa_2_all_pages__docx.md` showing that explicit unit exponents are part of the house style (`11+21=31`, alongside `32+42=52` and `33+43+53=63`). Combined with the page-75 rules `23 followed by one zero` and `each raised to the third power`, plus the damaged OCR token `g4g25171`, this stabilized the compact Quantum Prime numbering for 5040 as `24325171`, interpreted as `2^4 3^2 5^1 7^1`. Rebuilt the downstream artifacts accordingly: `pyth2_validated_workset.json` now has 9 stable items with counts `4 direct_validated / 5 corrected_source_validated`, and the holdout count drops from 4 to 3 because the page-75 notation item is no longer quarantined. Added `prime_numbering_5040` to the stable program layer in `pyth2_program_artifacts.json`, and `pyth2_validated_tables.json` now includes a `prime_numbering_table` with the stabilized row for 5040. The remaining unresolved Pyth-2 holdouts are only the three page-110 convention-sensitive items.
